title: The Macro Minute: Is Treasury Secretary Bessent right about the future of the Fed?

Ho ho ho. Merry Christmas Eve uh team
42. It's your skipper here Darius Dell
to present our macro minute for
Wednesday, December 24th, 2025. As
always, we'll start with the executive
summary from today's leadoff morning
note. So, let's dive right in. Today's
schemacro question is, is Treasury
Secretary Bessant right about the future
of the Fed? The short answer is yes. The
forward guidance he shared on the All-In
podcast this week is near perfectly
aligned with our datadriven, robustly
researched call for structural reforms
at the Fed, an outcome we've been
explicitly forecasting and helping our
global investor community profit from
for several years. Terms of analytical
nuance, the mere existence of the
Bitcoin asset class suggests we're not
alone in understanding that the US
fiscal authority is staunchly committed
to proflegate spending and chronic
undertaxation.
Nor are we alone in anticipating that
the US monetary authority will continue
to treat the US government's debt
disease with greater and greater
quantities of financial repression and
monetary [clears throat] debasement.
Where we are differentiated is in the
breadth and depth of the institutional
grade insights required to convince many
of the world's most respected
institutional investors that paradigm D
i.e. would wall street later dubbed the
debasement trade is coming and that they
should amend their core asset
allocations to feature gold in lie of
bonds because of it. It's one thing to
tweet out chart crimes that would get
you laughed out of every meeting on
global Wall Street or bull porn
thumbnails designed to tickle the
amydala of gullible retail investors.
It's an entirely different thing
altogether to convince institutional
investors to move a meaningful portion
of the 30 trillion of capital that our
byside clients collectively manage into
the barbarous relic that is gold.
Indeed, it has been humbling to watch
major Wall Street institution after
major Wall Street institution heed our
call to amend their core asset
allocations to feature gold in lie of
bonds. In our view, this process is
likely to persist for at least the next
several years due to the still too low
allocations to gold most of these
institutions feature in their core asset
allocations, i.e. usually not more than
5 to 10% if that. Recall that in October
of 2024, we made the brave decision to
replace the 30% target allocation to
bonds with gold in our flagship KISS
model portfolio. For what it's worth,
gold hit another all-time high today,
rising above 45,500 uh per ounce. The US
dollar has been devalued by 70% versus
gold in 2025, the sharpest decline since
1979.
The top the three 42 macro forecast that
Secretary Besson's latest for guidance
supported are number one, from January
2022, the Fed will revise its arbitrary
2% inflation target higher. Number two,
from September of 2023, the Fed's
independence will be gradually eroded
over time as it is forced to plug the
geopolitically driven supply demand
imbalance in the US Treasury bond market
with increasing quantities of debt
monetization and the schemes used to
justify it. For what it's worth, reserve
management operation purchases is one
such scheme. There will likely be
others, including yield curve control.
And finally, number three, in April of
2025, the Trump administration and the
Fed will run the US economy hot. So,
thank you, Treasury Secretary Scott
Besson, former client. I appreciate you
for uh for supporting our our themes and
more importantly supporting our
portfolios for our global investor
community. As always, members of our
global uh investor community can trust
that our institutional grade risk
management overlays, KISS and Dr. MO
will help our portfolios navigate these
emergent risks better than investors
attempting to manage risk without
systematic rules or relying on
fundamental research alone.
Transitioning to our 42 micro dashboard
as always to wrap up with a question
from our community. This was not a
question but a comment uh regarding our
macro class. So says uh the 42 macro
class is a much mustwatch for all. Uh

are tested and so on. I can't recommend

have to rewatch it just for the sheer
amount of alpha in this presentation.

with point A being, you know, novice
retail investor that makes, you know,
novice retail investor mistakes to point
B being institutional investor that does
not make any more retail investor
mistakes. Uh if you can just go from
point A to point B, you don't have to
get any further than point B. If you get
from point A to point B, you will retire
on time and comfortably. There's a CDE E
and FG after that too that you need to
get to if you want to be, you know, me.
But you don't have to be me to retire on
time and comfortably. You just have to
have the confidence to ex to
dispassionately execute our KISS model
portfolio for a long enough period of
time to uh maximize upside capture in
enough bull markets and minimize
downside capture in enough bare markets
so that your portfolio has a whole lot
more dollars in it uh uh at some future
point where you decide to retire and or
go to the great beyond and give your
kids to leave your kids a nice uh gift
then. But hopefully no one's going to
the great beyond today. Uh merry
